channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela earthquakes tops 1,400
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes rose above 1,400 on Saturday as foreign rescue teams poured into the country and authorities pressed on with the search for survivors in the hardest-hit coastal areas. The updated toll came as rescuers fanned out across La Guaira and par Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Ukraine has attacked bus carrying schoolchildren: Russia
item.description: Russia accused Ukraine of conducting a deadly drone strike on a bus carrying Belarusian schoolchildren on Wednesday, an allegation that ​Ukraine's military said was "false". Yegor Kovalchuk, the acting governor of Russia's ‌Bryansk region, which borders Ukraine, said the bus had been taking a Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Italian mother takes on Meta, TikTok after daughter's death
item.description:
In the span of just a few months, Irene Roggero Ugues watched her daughter Rossella's behaviour change as social media fed her an increasing stream of self-harm content, before the 12-year-old died by suicide.
Only after Rossella's death did Irene and her husband unlock her devices. They f Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russian artist known as Putin critic shot dead in eastern Poland
item.description: A Russian artist and cartoonist known for mocking Russian President Vladimir Putin was shot dead in eastern Poland on Monday in what Polish media are describing as a possible "execution-style" killing, reports aa.com.tr. The victim has been identified by several Polish broadcasters as Semyon Skrep Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: French watchdog orders conservative news channel CNews to comply with pluralism rules
item.description: France's media ‌regulator on Monday ordered rolling news channel CNews to comply with rules on pluralism and diversity of opinion, putting the ultra-conservative channel owned by billionaire Vincent Bollore one ​step away from disciplinary action. The channel, which critics have likened to th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: German finance minister proposes income tax cuts, Spiegel reports
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil has presented coalition negotiators with two proposals for ​an income tax reform, including tax ‌relief of up to €20 billion ($21.6 billion) and a higher levy on top earners, Spiegel reported on Monday.One ​proposal would provide moderate relief for Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10 million
item.description: Swiss voters decide on Sunday whether to back a proposal to cap the country's population in a referendum likened to Britain's Brexit vote, which could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and Bern's relations with the European Union. Driven by concern about immigration, pressure on publi Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools, joining trend of shelving screens for students
item.description:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year as part of a broad, international reversal on the use of screens in classrooms.
Since 2023, the Scandinavian country's center-rig Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Russian drone attack kills four in Ukraine's Kyiv region, officials say
item.description: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in a region surrounding the Ukrainian capital Kyiv has killed four people and injured seven more, Ukrainian officials said on Friday. The attack destroyed an administrative building and damaged two cars at the site, the regional governor Mykola Kalashnyk s Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Norway recovers porcelain, chandeliers from 18th century shipwreck
item.description: Archaeologists have recovered a trove of Chinese porcelain and European-made goods from a recently discovered 18th-century shipwreck off the coast of Norway, government and museum officials said on Monday. Among the goods found in the remains of the unnamed sailing ship were tightly stacked white Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Denmark's Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister
item.description: Denmark's Social Democratic leader Mette Frederiksen said on Monday she has agreed to form a centre-left coalition government, maintaining her grip on power amid a crisis in ties with US President Donald Trump over the future of Greenland. The deal to form a minority cabinet gives Frederiksen a th Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Tens of thousands march in Madrid to demand prime minister's resignation
item.description: At least seven police were injured in clashes with protesters, officials said, as tens of thousands of people marched through Madrid on Saturday, demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez after a series of corruption scandals. A small group of protesters tried to break through barr Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: US carries out fresh strikes against Iran, escalating hostilities
item.description: The U.S. military said it struck Iran again, hours after a tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz, in the worst escalation since the ​two sides signed an interim peace deal two weeks ago. Each of the warring sides has accused the other of violating the agreement reached two weeks ago to end the fo Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Strong earthquake hits Hindu Kush, shaking Afghanistan and Pakistan
item.description: A strong earthquake struck Afghanistan’s Hindu Kush region on Saturday, the European Mediterranean Seismological Centre said, sending out tremors that could be felt from the capital Kabul across the border into neighbouring Pakistan. People ran out of their doors in panic in Swat district in Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thousands of Hungarians join first Budapest Pride march since Orban's defeat
item.description: More than 10,000 Hungarians on Saturday joined Budapest's first annual Pride march since right-wing leader Viktor Orban's ​election defeat in April, braving record heat in the city to walk ‌with huge rainbow and European Union flags. Last year's march, which Orban tried to ban as part of his Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Venezuela welcomes 1,600 foreign rescuers in urgent search for quake survivors
item.description: Venezuela's government said on Saturday 1,600 members of foreign rescue teams had arrived to help search for survivors of the devastating twin earthquakes which killed more than 900 people this week ​as it tightened access to the worst-affected state. Residents and volunteers in La Guaira, a popul Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for Iran peace talks with Hormuz in spotlight
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: State of emergency declared in Bolivia
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee is unlawful, US judge rules
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump appoints ally and FHFA chief Pulte as acting US intelligence director
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Rubio to testify before Congress for the first time since the start of the Iran war
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 11 confirmed dead in Washington state chemical accident
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump to headline 250th anniversary fair opening after performers drop out
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pentagon chief sounds 'alarm' over China's buildup, urges allies to boost defence spend
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Suspect killed during shooting near White House
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: New York Times sues Pentagon again over media access restrictions
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mexico denies CNN report on deadly CIA operations against cartels
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crash kills 14
item.description: A helicopter belonging to Saudi oil giant Aramco crashed on Sunday in Ras Tanura on Saudi Arabia's eastern coast on the Gulf, west of the Strait of Hormuz, killing 14 nationals, the state news agency reported, adding that the cause was unknown. Aramco had resumed crude oil loadings on Friday at it Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Eight killed in Pakistani military operations in Balochistan
item.description: Pakistan’s military says that it has killed eight “terrorists” in intelligence operations in Balochistan’s Kharan and Mastung districts, reports Pakistani daily Dawn. The country’s Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) said it had conducted the “intelligence-dr Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 700 civilians killed by Myanmar Army in 6 months
item.description: Myanmar's military killed more than 700 civilians in six months, including hundreds of women and children, according to a UN report cited by the BBC. The report from the UN Human Rights Office, covering August to January -- the six months from when the military announced elections -- verified a mi Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: North Korea's Kim says country will exercise its position as nuclear state
item.description: Tata Electronics said on Monday it had detected a recent "cybersecurity incident", after researchers said World Leaks posted purported component design and specification papers of Apple and Tesla, both customers of the Indian group. The ransomware group has posted more than 200,000 files on the da Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Rebel TMC camp ousts Mamata, suspends Abhishek
item.description: Politics in West Bengal has taken a dramatic turn as the faction led by Uluberia Purba MLA Ritabrata Banerjee, calling itself the "real Trinamool", announced the suspension of party national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee and removed Mamata Banerjee from the post of party chairperson. The dev Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Fire at coaching centre in India's Lucknow kills at least eight, police say
item.description: At least eight people were killed in a fire at a coaching centre in Lucknow city in northern India on Monday, a local police officer said. Local media said the death toll was higher and put it at 14, adding that they were mostly students. Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: China building ‘world’s biggest dam’ as India plans counter
item.description: China has begun work on the world’s largest hydroelectric dam, situated on the lower reaches of Tibet’s Yarlung Tsangpo River, about 50km from the Indian border, reports Indian broadcaster NDTV. In response, India is also pushing forward with an initiative of its own – a 11,000MW Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Pakistani Kashmir faces shutdown as protests leave more than 20 dead
item.description: A territory-wide shutdown in Pakistan-administered Kashmir has brought daily life to a standstill after the region's deadliest unrest in years left at least 24 people dead in nearly two weeks of protests. The confrontation between local authorities and supporters of the recently banned Joint Awami Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thailand revives $30bn coast-to-coast corridor to rival Malacca Strait
item.description: Chaiyaporn Arunrasamee hunched over his fishing nets, overlooking the waters of the Andaman Sea, where Thailand's government is proposing an ambitious "Land Bridge" that will ferry goods between ports on opposite sides of the peninsula. "Personally, I don't want it to happen at all," Chaiyaporn sa Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Funeral for Khamenei to begin July 4, burial set for July 9
item.description: Funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will begin in Tehran on July 4 and conclude with his burial in the northeastern city of Mashhad on July 9, state media reported on Saturday. Khamenei was killed in Israeli and US strikes on Iran in February. His death marked the end of more than Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: India names Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth as next chief of army staff
item.description: India has appointed Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth as the next chief of the army staff, with the veteran officer set to assume command on Jun 30 following the retirement of General Upendra Dwivedi. According to a statement from the Ministry of Defence cited by Hindustan Times, President Droupadi Murmu approve Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Thai king's eldest daughter dies, aged 47, after long illness, Royal Palace says
item.description: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, the eldest child of Thai King Maha Vajiralongkorn, has died at 47, the royal palace said on Friday, after multiple health problems and nearly four years in a coma. The princess was hospitalised in December 2022 after a sudden loss of consciou Delivered by PolitePaul service
